perturbation.original_text: ALL RIGHT TITLE AND INTEREST IN AND TO THE CONFIDENTI AL INFORMATION SHALL REMAIN WITH DISCLOSING PARTY OR ITS LICENSORS. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROV IDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON - INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.changed_text: ALL RIGHT TITLE AND INTEREST IN AND TO THE CONFIDENTI AL INFORMATION SHALL REMAIN WITH DISCLOSING PARTY OR ITS LICENSORS. Nothing in this Agreement is intended to grant any rights to Recipient under any patents, copyrights, trademarks, or trade secrets of Disclosing Party. 4. The obligations and limitations set forth herein regarding Confident ial Information shall not apply to information which is: (a) at any time in the public domain, other than by a breach on the part of the Recipient; or (b) at any time rightfully received from a third party which had the right to and transmits it to the Rec ipient without any obligation of confidentiality. ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION IS PROV IDED "AS IS". THE DISCLOSING PARTY MAKES NO WARRANTIES, EXPRESS, IMPLIED OR OTHERWISE, REGARDING NON - INFRINGEMENT OF THIRD PARTY RIGHTS OR ITS ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS OR PERFORMANCE.
perturbation.explanation: By interjecting section 4 into section 3, the warranty disclaimer gets awkwardly positioned. Colorado law requires clarity and conspicuousness for disclaimers of warranties. Placing an exception to what information is considered confidential in the middle of a warranty disclaimer could make the disclaimer less conspicuous and harder to understand.
perturbation.law_explanation: Colorado law requires that disclaimers of warranty be conspicuous. Inserting unrelated clauses into the middle of the warranty disclaimer makes it less conspicuous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that Colorado law requires clarity and conspicuousness for disclaimers of warranties. The law explanation states that inserting unrelated clauses into the middle of the warranty disclaimer makes it less conspicuous and potentially unenforceable. The original contract text presents a standard disclaimer of warranties, whereas the changed text inserts an exception regarding confidential information into the middle of that disclaimer. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing and Scraped Law Snippet 2 only contains login information for LexisNexis, which is unhelpful, the Perturbation and Law explanations outline a plausible contradiction based on the structural flaw of the disclaimer being less conspicuous and clear.

perturbation.explanation: Moving the exception to confidential information ahead of the warranty disclaimer buries the disclaimer deeper into the contract, making it less conspicuous and potentially more difficult to enforce. Colorado law emphasizes conspicuousness for warranty disclaimers.
perturbation.law_explanation: Colorado law requires disclaimers of warranty to be conspicuous. By burying the disclaimer after a lengthy exception, its conspicuousness is reduced, potentially rendering it unenforceable.

perturbation.changed_text: The validity, construction and enforceability of this Agreement shall be gover ned in all respects by the law of the state of Colorado . This Agreement shall control in the event of a conflict with any other agreement betwee n the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the date first above written. Disclosing Party: Company: _______________________ Name: __________________________ Role: ___________________________ Phone Number: __________________ Signature: ___________________________ ____ The Recipient : Company: _____ Pitch Deck Fire ______ Name: _______ Stacie Shaw __________ Role: _________ Founder ___________ Phone Number: __ _214-770-9216 ____ Signature: _______________________________ This Agreement may not be amended except in writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By moving the 'no amendment except in writing' clause to the end, after the signatures, there is a potential argument that it wasn't agreed upon as part of the original agreement because it's no longer clearly within the body of the agreement before signatures. This could contradict general contract law principles of mutual assent in Colorado.
perturbation.contradicted_law: James H. Moore & Associates Realty, Inc. v. Arrowhead at Vail, 892 P.2d 367 (Colo. App. 1994)
perturbation.law_citation: James H. Moore & Associates Realty, Inc. v. Arrowhead at Vail, 892 P.2d 367 (Colo. App. 1994)
perturbation.law_explanation: Moving the amendment clause after the signatures could create ambiguity regarding mutual assent to that specific term. Colorado law requires mutual assent for a contract to be valid. Placement after the signature block could raise doubts about whether both parties intended to be bound by it.
perturbation.justification: The change moves the 'no amendment except in writing' clause after the signature block. The Perturbation Explanation argues that this could lead to an argument that the clause wasn't part of the original agreement. The Law Explanation supports this, stating Colorado law requires mutual assent for a contract to be valid and the placement after the signature block could raise doubts about whether both parties intended to be bound by it. While the scraped snippets are missing, the combined Perturbation and Law explanations point to a Structural Flaw Legal Contradiction, making the contract unenforceable due to ambiguity about mutual assent on a key term.
